title: CachingStore thundering herd on TTL expiry
description: Trigger: N async tasks share a CachingStore instance and all call get("same-key") after the TTL expires. Bad behavior: the lock is released before the inner.get() call (by design, to avoid holding lock across await), so all N tasks see a miss simultaneously, all call inner.get() in parallel, and all write the result. For AWS SM/SSM backends with API rate limits this burst can trigger throttling. Fix requires in-flight deduplication (e.g. a pending-fetch map: if a fetch is already in flight for a key, wait for it rather than starting a second one). Accepted tradeoff if users are typically single-task; should be documented as a known limitation if not fixed.
